name: Video Billboard
description: A video playback system that displays videos or images on mesh surfaces using VideoTexture. Supports both external URLs and internal library assets with automatic fallback. Features proximity-based volume control, aspect ratio preservation, autoplay, looping, and rotation. Ideal for in-game screens, billboards, and environmental video elements.

startOnTrigger.name: Start On Trigger
startOnTrigger.description: When enabled, video playback only starts when activated by a Trigger behavior instead of on load.

videoAsset.name: Video Asset
videoAsset.description: A video asset to display on the billboard.

external_url.name: Video URL
external_url.description: An URL pointing to an exteral video to display on the billboard.

internal_url.name: Video from Library (legacy)
internal_url.description: Select a video from the project's asset library to display on the billboard.

useLocalFile.name: Use asset over URL
useLocalFile.description: Legacy toggle. When enabled, prefers internal_url over external_url. Superseded by videoAsset.

rotate.name: Rotate video (in degrees)
rotate.description: Rotates the video texture on the mesh surface by the specified angle in degrees (-360 to 360).

aspect.name: Geometry aspect ratio
aspect.description: The aspect ratio of the target mesh geometry, used to correctly fit the video texture without distortion.

fit.name: Fit
fit.description: How the video fits the mesh: Contain letterboxes to show the full video, Cover fills the surface and may crop edges.

autoplay.name: Auto play
autoplay.description: When enabled, the video starts playing automatically as soon as it loads.

loop.name: Loop video
loop.description: When enabled, the video restarts from the beginning after it finishes playing.

proximity.name: Proximity distance
proximity.description: The maximum distance from the player at which the video will play. Volume fades as the player moves further away.

volume.name: Initial volume
volume.description: The base volume level for video audio (0.0 to 1.0). Actual volume scales with player proximity.

muted.name: Mute audio
muted.description: When enabled, the video plays without audio. Also helps bypass browser autoplay restrictions.

documentation:
## Video Billboard

A video playback system that displays videos or images on mesh surfaces using VideoTexture. Supports both external URLs and internal library assets with automatic fallback. Features proximity-based volume control, aspect ratio preservation, autoplay, looping, and rotation. Ideal for in-game screens, billboards, and environmental video elements.

### Key Features
- Video playback from URL or local library
- Automatic fallback: tries alternative source on load failure
- Aspect ratio preservation with cover/contain modes
- Configurable video rotation (degrees)
- Proximity-based volume control (distance-based fade)
- Autoplay, loop, and muted options
- Trigger-based play/stop control
- Muted autoplay fallback for browser restrictions
- VideoTexture with needsUpdate management
- ScalingImageMaterial for proper aspect ratio fitting
- Original material restoration on cleanup
- WebGL texture optimization
- Editor live preview with attribute updates

### How It Works
1. Attach to mesh object (or parent with mesh child)
2. Configure video source:
   - external_url: HTTP/HTTPS video URL
   - internal_url: video from project library (enum)
   - useLocalFile: prioritizes internal over external
3. Set playback options:
   - autoplay: starts immediately on load
   - loop: repeats video endlessly
   - muted: disables audio (helps with autoplay restrictions)
   - volume: initial volume level (0.0-1.0)
4. Configure display:
   - aspect: geometry aspect ratio for fitting
   - fit: 'cover' (fill) or 'contain' (letterbox)
   - rotate: rotation angle in degrees (-360 to 360)
5. Set proximity (optional):
   - Distance threshold for proximity-based playback
   - Volume fades based on distance to player
6. On add:
   - Stores original material for restoration
   - Constructs video URL (prioritizes based on useLocalFile)
   - Validates URL format (http/https protocols)
   - Creates VideoSource instance
7. VideoSource initialization:
   - Creates HTMLVideoElement
   - Loads video metadata (width, height)
   - Creates THREE.VideoTexture from element
8. Material creation:
   - Calculates aspect ratio: video / geometry
   - ScalingImageMaterial.createMaterial(texture, aspect, rotation)
   - Replaces mesh material with video material
9. Playback control:
   - Autoplay: calls play() after video ready
   - Trigger: responds to 'activate'/'deactivate' messages
10. Update loop:
    - Sets texture.needsUpdate = true when playing
    - Proximity check: distance to player
    - Volume calculation: distanceRatio * initialVolume
    - Auto play/pause based on proximity threshold
11. Fallback handling:
    - On load error, tries alternative source
    - Shows toast notifications for errors
    - Attempts muted autoplay on NotAllowedError
    - Falls back to gray placeholder material on total failure
12. Cleanup/dispose:
    - Pauses or stops video
    - Restores original material
    - Releases video resources (commented out - TODO)

### Common Use Cases
- In-game TV screens or monitors
- Billboard advertisements in cities
- Tutorial videos on surfaces
- Security camera feeds
- Cinematic screens in theaters
- Video textures for environmental storytelling
- Interactive kiosks with video content
- Holographic displays
- News screens in sci-fi environments
- Music videos in nightclub scenes

### When Not To Use
- For skybox videos (use different approach)
- When video should be 2D UI overlay (use HTML video element)
- For 360-degree videos (needs a different material path)
- When video needs transparency/alpha channel
- For procedural video effects (use shaders)
- When multiple videos should sync precisely
- For streaming live video feeds (needs media server)
- When video needs custom material effects
- For VR stereo video (needs special format)
- When video should be interactive/clickable (needs raycast)

### Technical Details
- VideoSource class: manages HTMLVideoElement and VideoTexture
- Video loading: async createVideoSource() returns promise
- Width/height: videoSource.getWidth()/getHeight() from video element
- Aspect ratio: (videoWidth / videoHeight) vs (geometryAspect)
- Fit modes: cover (imgAspect/geoAspect), contain (geoAspect/imgAspect)
- Rotation: converted to radians (degrees * PI / 180)
- ScalingImageMaterial: custom node material for aspect-aware scaling
- Texture update: texture.needsUpdate = true on playing frames
- Proximity distance: distanceTo(playerPosition) each frame
- Distance ratio: max(0, min(1, 1 - distance / proximityDistance))
- Volume calculation: distanceRatio * initialVolume
- URL validation: new URL(string) with protocol check
- Fallback logic: catch block tries opposite source (external->internal)
- Toast notifications: showToast() for user feedback
- Play restrictions: NotAllowedError triggers muted autoplay attempt
- Browser autoplay policy: muted videos more likely to autoplay
- Video element: injected into document.body
- Renderer: passed to VideoSource for texture creation
- Material replacement: mesh.material = videoMaterial
- Original material: stored in originalMaterial property
- Cleanup flag: wasPlayingBeforeDispose for editor attribute updates
- State tracking: isPlaying boolean
- Editor updates: full dispose and reinit on URL change
- Partial update: only material recreation if URL unchanged
- Trigger support: startOnTrigger flag + onEvent() handler
- Throttle: MEDIUM priority, frustum/distance culling enabled
- TODO: Proper video resource release (commented out)
